```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id
{
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD)
}

lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= NumDigits(x.value)
  ensures x.width >= PAD ==> Canonicalize(x).width == x.width
  ensures x.width < PAD ==> Canonicalize(x).width == PAD
{ }
```

**Explanation of the `ensures` clauses:**

- `kind` and `value` are preserved — canonicalization never changes which entity the id refers to.
- `width >= PAD` — the result is left-padded to a minimum of 4 digits.
- `width >= NumDigits(x.value)` — the result is still well-formed (its width can hold its value); this follows from the others but is stated for completeness.
- `x.width >= PAD ==> width unchanged` — ids already at 4+ digits are returned unchanged (and this makes canonicalizing a canonical id a no-op).
- `x.width < PAD ==> width == PAD` — narrower ids are widened exactly to PAD, not more.

The last two clauses together pin down the result width exactly as a function of the input, leaving no ambiguity.
